summary: In this interview, Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw discusses her journey as the founder of Biocon, emphasizing the importance of purpose-driven business, ethical leadership, and taking bold risks. She shares insights on overcoming gender bias, the necessity of failure for growth, and her approach to inclusive, problem-solving leadership.

principles.statement: Business must be led with a strong sense of purpose beyond just making money.
principles.rationale: If you start a business solely for money, you will lose your sense of purpose and eventually lose your employees. Purpose drives leadership and creates global impact.
principles.source_quote: I always believe pramod that um business is about purpose and I think every one of us who runs a business have to have a sense of purpose

principles.statement: Denying access to life-saving therapies based on affordability is inhuman.
principles.rationale: The pharmaceutical industry is a humanitarian business meant to save lives. Innovation must serve the people who need it most, leveraging economies of scale to drive affordability.
principles.source_quote: if you deny access to patients on the basis of affordability I think that's inhuman

principles.statement: Failure is a stepping stone to success.
principles.rationale: Failures open up new opportunities you never thought possible. Overcoming a failure gives you the courage to move on and take bigger risks.
principles.source_quote: I always believe that failure is you know a stepping stone to success

principles.statement: Profits must be generated ethically and with empathy.
principles.rationale: Generating profits at any cost destroys value and leads to public crucifixion, as seen with environmental disasters or the opioid crisis. Mainstream companies must focus on shareholder returns, but with strict social responsibility.
principles.source_quote: You cannot generate profits at any cost... when you practice your business business with social purpose you can be very profitable but extremely ethical

principles.statement: Work-life balance is achieved through daily prioritization, not a static equilibrium.
principles.rationale: You balance your life by deciding what is most important to you on any given day, whether that means working until midnight for a crucial meeting or sacrificing a business conference for a family milestone.
principles.source_quote: this whole phrase of work life balance is very very flawed because I believe that everyone can have a work-life balance if you know how to prioritize your life

frameworks.name: Raising Capital for Novel Ideas
frameworks.when_to_apply: When pitching a new, risky, or unconventional business idea to investors.
steps: Articulate your idea clearly so people understand what you are trying to do.
Dimension the value of your idea by explaining the market opportunity.
Demonstrate how you differentiate yourself and address large unmet needs.
Find investors who share your excitement and understand the vision.
Build a track record of delivering on your promises to secure future funding.
frameworks.source_quote: if you can articulate the value of your idea the the opportunity the market opportunity of your idea and how you're basically trying to make a difference or to differentiate yourself and addressing very large unmet needs then I think people understand what you're trying to do and will invest in you

mental_models.name: Problem-Solving Leadership
mental_models.description: A leadership style that frames work as problems to be solved rather than instructions to be executed. This empowers employees, builds their confidence, and motivates them to take on bigger challenges.
mental_models.example: Instead of giving a directive, she tells her team: 'here guys this is the problem try and solve it'.

mental_models.name: The Lab-to-Market Journey
mental_models.description: A lens for understanding gestational risk in the biotech sector, recognizing that it takes 5 to 7 years of high uncertainty to bring an idea to market, requiring 'catalytic capital' and patience unlike digital IT startups.

heuristics: Follow the science, and the regulations will follow.
Start business relationships on the belief of trust, and course-correct only when that trust is broken.
Encourage dissent; tell your team not to agree with everything you say.
If you're a scientist, dream big and don't think there is a gender bias in science.

signature_quotes.text: I always believe pramod that um business is about purpose and I think every one of us who runs a business have to have a sense of purpose
signature_quotes.context: Discussing her core philosophy for starting and running Biocon.

signature_quotes.text: if I follow the science the regulations will follow
signature_quotes.context: Explaining her bold bet on biosimilars before US and European regulatory guidelines even existed.

signature_quotes.text: this whole phrase of work life balance is very very flawed because I believe that everyone can have a work-life balance if you know how to prioritize your life
signature_quotes.context: Answering a question about the myth that women cannot balance family and leadership.

signature_quotes.text: Don't think you're smarter than women because women are as smart. Learn to respect that and the moment you accept women as being as smart as you I think the world will be a different place
signature_quotes.context: Her closing message to the men in the audience regarding gender bias.

signature_quotes.text: I always tell people don't agree with everything I say I may be wrong so be free to tell me that you don't agree with me

anti_patterns.description: Starting a business solely to make money.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: You will lose your sense of purpose and eventually lose your employees, as people need a deeper motivation to stay engaged.
It stifles problem-solving, discourages dissent, and fails to empower or motivate teams to take on bigger challenges.

anti_patterns.description: Generating profits at any cost without ethical considerations.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It destroys long-term value and leads to public backlash and corporate crucifixion, as seen in environmental disasters and the opioid crisis.

anti_patterns.description: Tolerating gray areas in data integrity or intellectual property.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Allowing even a little bit of digression sends the wrong signal and seeps into the corporate culture, ultimately destroying the core value proposition of the business.
